title: Shock and confusion in a 38-year-old with fatigue
presentation: A 38-year-old female with a 6-month history of fatigue, weight loss, and skin darkening presents with confusion, severe abdominal pain, and vomiting for 24 hours after a URI. Vitals: BP 78/50, HR 136, RR 20, Temp 38.3C, SpO2 97%. Exam: diffuse hyperpigmentation especially in skin creases and gums, confused, dry mucous membranes, no focal neurology.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Do NOT wait for cortisol results before giving hydrocortisone in suspected Addisonian crisis — it is life-threatening. Hyperpigmentation + hypotension + hyponatremia + hyperkalemia = primary adrenal insufficiency. Precipitant was infection.
